title: Congee with Century Egg

seo.title: Century Egg Congee Recipe: Authentic Chinese Breakfast
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Century Egg Congee at home with our easy, step-by-step Chinese recipe. Perfect for a comforting and savory breakfast!
longTailKeywords: how to make Century Egg Congee
Century Egg Congee step by step
lowCompetitionKeywords: Chinese breakfast porridge with century egg
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Century Egg Congee
Pro Tips for the Best Century Egg Congee
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Chinese Century Egg Congee, garnished with green onions and ginger, ready to be served.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Century Egg Congee, a classic Chinese breakfast.

description: This comforting Century Egg Congee is a beloved Chinese breakfast, featuring silky smooth rice porridge simmered with savory century eggs. Its unique flavor profile and creamy texture make it a deeply satisfying and nourishing dish, perfect for a chilly morning or a light meal. Experience the authentic taste of traditional Chinese home cooking with this classic recipe.

ingredients.item: Pork tenderloin or lean ground pork

instructions.title: Prepare the Rice
instructions.text: Rinse the jasmine rice thoroughly until the water runs clear. For a creamier congee, you can soak the rice in water for 30 minutes to an hour before cooking. In a large pot, combine the rinsed rice with 8-10 cups of water or broth. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat to low.

instructions.title: Simmer the Congee Base
instructions.text: Cover the pot and let the congee simmer gently for at least 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking. The rice grains should break down and the mixture should thicken into a creamy porridge. If it becomes too thick, add more hot water or broth as needed.

instructions.title: Prepare Pork and Century Eggs
instructions.text: While the congee simmers, slice the pork tenderloin thinly or mince it. Marinate the pork with soy sauce and cornstarch for at least 10 minutes. Peel the century eggs and dice them into small pieces. Julienne the ginger and chop the green onions.

instructions.title: Add Pork and Century Eggs
instructions.text: Once the congee has reached your desired consistency, add the marinated pork to the pot. Stir well to separate the pork slices and cook through, about 2-3 minutes. Then, add most of the diced century eggs and half of the julienned ginger to the congee. Reserve some century egg and ginger for garnish.

instructions.title: Season and Finish
instructions.text: Season the congee with salt and white pepper to taste. Continue to simmer for another 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Stir gently to ensure nothing sticks to the bottom of the pot. The congee should be smooth and rich.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Century Egg Congee into individual bowls. Garnish each serving with the remaining diced century egg, julienned ginger, and chopped green onions. Drizzle with a few drops of sesame oil just before serving for an extra layer of aroma and flavor. Enjoy immediately.

tips: For extra creamy congee, freeze the rinsed rice for a few hours or overnight before cooking. The freezing process breaks down the rice grains faster.
If you prefer a richer flavor, use homemade chicken broth instead of water. You can also add a pinch of dried scallops or a few pieces of dried shiitake mushrooms (soaked and sliced) for umami.

Congee is best served fresh. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or broth to thin it out if needed.
article: Congee with Century Egg is a beloved Chinese Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
